data.title: Nvidia CEO Huang joins X, signs letter to Congress on open AI models
data.description:
Nvidia (NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang has joined the X (formerly Twitter) social network, the tech titan said on Friday.

In his first post, he revealed that he signed a letter to the U.S. Congress on open AI models and the need for American leadership in the space.

“AI will transform every industry, power every company, and be built by every country,” Huang wrote in his post. “Open models strengthen safety and cybersecurity, accelerate innovation and diffusion, and enable sovereignty. The world needs both frontier closed models and frontier open models.”

The three-page letter was signed by Nvidia, along with a host of other tech industry heavyweights, including Meta Platforms (META), Box (BOX), CrowdStrike (CRWD), Microsoft (MSFT), and others.

Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella also posted on X about the letter and said they are "essential to a healthy AI ecosystem," while adding they will "strengthen American competitiveness,"  expand economic opportunity, and protect national security.

“Our AI leadership will be judged not by one frontier AI model, but by whether the United States builds a strong, open ecosystem that diffuses into every sector,” the letter reads. “This is essential for creating opportunities for innovation and prosperity across the country. It requires expanding access to AI, encouraging competition, robust application layers, and giving Americans greater control over the technology they rely on. Open-weight models—AI models that anyone can download, inspect, modify, and run on their own infrastructure—are an important part of that foundation because they make advanced AI more accessible, adaptable, and widely available.”

This is breaking news...

data.title: 4 stocks to watch on Friday: VZ, LLY, ORCL, DELL
data.description:
Stock index futures were higher on Friday as the stock market hoped to recover from Thursday's selloff.

Here are four stocks to watch on the day: 

Oracle (ORCL) shares rose 2.33% in premarket trading after the software and cloud company secured a 10-year contract with the U.S. Department of War to provide software services in a deal worth up to $6.99 billion if all option years are exercised. The contract covers Oracle software for on-premise data centers across all branches of the U.S. military, the Coast Guard, and the U.S. intelligence community.

Verizon Communications (VZ) shares fell 0.94% in early trading after the telecom giant reported mixed second-quarter results. Adjusted earnings topped Wall Street estimates, while revenue came in below expectations. Verizon also raised its full-year guidance for the second consecutive quarter, citing strong subscriber growth and record profitability.

Dell Technologies (DELL) shares rose 0.95% in premarket trading after Citi placed the technology company on a 90-day upside catalyst watch ahead of its July-quarter earnings report. The brokerage said enterprise IT infrastructure spending continues to strengthen, supported by artificial intelligence investments, server refresh cycles, and broader demand for data center capacity.

Eli Lilly (LLY) shares fell 0.24% in early trading after Novo Nordisk said it filed a preliminary injunction in a U.S. federal court seeking to immediately block Lilly from running advertisements for its obesity and diabetes medicines. The filing comes days after Novo sued its U.S. rival, alleging Lilly's advertising campaign for its GLP-1 products misled American consumers

data.title: Newmont posts Q2 earnings topper despite lower gold prices
data.description:
Newmont (NEM) up 1.3% pre-market Friday after reporting better than expected Q2 adjusted earnings and revenue rose 15% Y/Y to $6.12B even as its average gold prices fell compared to Q1.

Q2 profit rose to $2.2B, or $2.06/share, from $2.06B, or $1.85/share, in the year-earlier quarter; adjusted earnings came in at $2.10/share, above the $1.99 FactSet analyst consensus.

Newmont (NEM) said its Q2 average realized gold price rose to $4,414/oz from $3,320/oz in the year-ago quarter but fell sharply from its Q1 average selling price of $4,900, as gold rallied early in the year.

The company said its attributable gold production fell to 1.29M oz from 1.48M oz, hurt ​by lower output at Cadia due to seismic events, and at Ahafo South, Penasquito, and Yanacocha due to lower grades from planned mine sequencing.

Newmont (NEM) said it remains on track to meet its full-year gold production guidance of 5.3M oz while expecting Q3 output to be ⁠broadly in line with Q2, as the Cadia operations returned to normal levels as of mid-June.

Newmont (NEM) hopes for a resolution but is prepared to take legal action against Barrick Mining (B) if the companies cannot resolve a dispute over the future of the Nevada Gold Mines joint venture, CEO Natascha Viljoen said on the company's earnings conference call.

"Operations remain firmly on track for guidance, with stronger-than-expected performance at Lihir and Yanacocha pulling ~50K oz from H2 into Q2," BMO analyst Matthew Murphy said in a note, maintaining his Outperform rating.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway closes on $6.8B Taylor Morrison deal
data.description:
Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) (BRK.A) completed its acquisition of Taylor Morrison for $72.50 per common share in cash, the company said Friday, a move that adds to its Clayton Properties homebuilding operations.

The acquisition represents a total equity value of $6.8B for Taylor Morrison and a total enterprise value of ~$8.5B.

Berkshire's bolstered homebuilding business will serve renters, entry-level, move-up, and resort lifestyle segments, the company said. Combined, Taylor Morrison and Clayton Properties Group delivered almost 23,000 site-built home closings in 2025, operate in 21 states and 52 housing markets, and serve more than 700 communities nationally—positioning the combined business as the fourth-largest U.S. homebuilder.

"This best-in-class national homebuilder will lead our vision for a unified site-built homebuilding operation," said Berkshire Hathaway's Chief Executive Officer Greg Abel.

Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.B) B shares rose 0.1% in Friday premarket trading, while  A shares (BRK.A) increased 0.5%.

data.title: AMD in spotlight as bullish AI event praised by Wall Street
data.description:
AMD (AMD) was in the spotlight on Friday as the company's Advancing AI event was more bullish than Wall Street expected, leading to praise from several analysts.

Shares rose 1% in premarket trading, due in part to better-than-expected results and guidance  from competitor Intel (INTC). 

“We are encouraged by the technical advantages of AMD’s Helios AI rack-scale solution, which formally launched at the Advancing AI show,” Roth analyst Suji Desilva wrote in a note to clients. “We believe the company’s family of Instinct AI GPUs, Venice CPUs, and networking products provide flexible and scalable support for many large hyperscale and frontier AI company customers. We raise our price target from $500 to $650 and maintain our Buy.”

GF Securities Jeff Pu said the company raising the size of the total addressable market for AI accelerators (now at $1.4T by 2030) and server CPUs (now surpassing $220B by 2030) is a sign the company is executing well. “This is consistent with our constructive stance that the AI cycle [is still intact] despite the recent market unwind,” Pu wrote in a note to clients.

Pu, who has a Buy rating and a $640 price target on AMD, also said he was impressed with the MI455X, calling it a “big leap,” and sees it narrowing the gap with Nvidia's (NVDA) offerings. For the upcoming MI500, Pu said he expects it to be “ahead of [Nvidia's] Rubin Ultra in hardware due to a 4-die package, larger HBM capacity and larger first-layer scale-up domain.”

Lastly, AMD's Venice CPU is the most capable CPU for AI workloads and believes the average selling price could rise between 30% and 35% year-over-year in 2027.

Wedbush Securities analyst Matt Bryson said this week's announcement that Anthropic (ANTHRO) is buying up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's MI450 systems is a sign the company is “well positioned to meet/exceed goals through the second half of this year and into (if not through) 2027.”

Baird analyst Tristan Gerra raised his price target to a Wall Street high of $1,250 from $625 and reiterated his Outperform rating, stating the event was a sign of “strong growth in both AMD’s AI GPU and AI CPU platforms.”

Additionally, looking towards next week, industry players such as Texas Instruments (TXN) and Valero (VLO) will see the ex-dividend dates for their upcoming dividend payments.

data.title: Merck signs licensing deals with companies including Viatris for new HIV pill
data.description:
Merck (MRK) on Friday announced seven licensing agreements with generic drug manufacturers, including Viatris (VTRS), to broaden access to its experimental HIV pill, alimatravir, in low- and middle-income countries.

Three companies from sub-Saharan Africa, including Aspen Pharmacare Holdings (APNHF) (APNHY), and four from India, including Viatris (VTRS), reached the royalty-free agreements, the New Jersey-based pharma giant said.

The non-exclusive and voluntary deals cover both public and private sectors in 129 countries that account for a majority number of new HIV cases, setting up early manufacturing capabilities to provide timely access to alimatravir following its approval, the company added.

The agreements come at a time when enrollments are underway for Phase 3 trials designed to evaluate the once-monthly oral pill for the prevention of HIV-1 as part of pre-exposure prophylaxis.

Merck (MRK) made the announcement ahead of the 26th International AIDS Conference taking place in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil, later this month.

data.title: C.H. Robinson hit with $604M Texas jury advisory verdict in trucking accident
data.description:
C.H. Robinson (CHRW) down 2% pre-market Friday after disclosing it could face as much as $604M in compensatory damages after a Texas jury issued an advisory verdict in a lawsuit tied to a trucking accident involving a contracted motor carrier.

The company said damages could be assessed against it, although the advisory verdict is subject to post-trial proceedings before the court enters a final verdict; it disagrees with the verdict and expects to appeal if the jury's verdict is final.

Three people were killed in the Mississippi crash alongside numerous injuries to other automobile passengers in a pileup that occurred after a Lupus Superior truck driven by its employee plowed into several cars; C.H. Robinson (CHRW) had hired Lupus Superior to move products.

The jury was not swayed by C.H. Robinson's (CHRW) defense that Lupus Superior had a satisfactory grade from the Federal Motor Carrier Safety Administration before and after the fatal crash.

data.title: Dyadic regains compliance with Nasdaq listing requirements
data.description:
• Dyadic International (DYAI) announced on Friday it had regained compliance with Nasdaq Listing Rules 5550(a)(2) and 5550(b).
  • The company's common stock will continue to be listed and traded on the Nasdaq Capital Market.
  • Shares -6.92%.

data.title: NanoViricides announces $3.8M registered direct offering; shares down
data.description:
• NanoViricides (NNVC) entered into a securities purchase agreement with a single institutional investor for a registered direct offering.
  • The offering includes 2.52M common shares (or pre-funded warrants) and accompanying warrants to purchase an additional 2.52M shares.
  • The transaction is expected to generate approximately $3.8M in gross proceeds before fees and expenses.
  • Each warrant has an exercise price of $1.75 per share and a 5.5-year term.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about July 27, 2026.
  • NNVC shares down 11% premarket.
